Package de.uka.ilkd.key.settings

Class StrategySettings

  • Method Details

    • getMaxSteps

      public int getMaxSteps()
      returns the maximal amount of heuristics steps before a user interaction is required
      Returns:
      the maximal amount of heuristics steps before a user interaction is required

    • setMaxSteps

      public void setMaxSteps(int mSteps)
      sets the maximal amount of heuristic steps before a user interaction is required
      Parameters:
      mSteps - maximal amount of heuristic steps

    • getStrategy

      public Name getStrategy()
      Get the name of the active strategy
      Returns:
      the name of the active strategy

    • setStrategy

      public void setStrategy(Name name)
      Set the name of the active strategy
      Parameters:
      name - the name of teh strategy

    • readSettings

      public void readSettings(Properties props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      gets a Properties object and has to perform the necessary steps in order to change this object in a way that it represents the stored settings

    • writeSettings

      public void writeSettings(Properties props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      The settings to store are written to the given Properties object.
      Parameters:
      props - the Properties object where to write the settings as (key, value) pair

    • readSettings

      public void readSettings(Configuration props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      This method transfers the given configuration information into the local states. The setter methods are used so PropertyChangeEvent should be triggered accordingly to the new state.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null references to a configuration object. The state of this object shall not be changed by the implementations.

    • writeSettings

      public void writeSettings(Configuration props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      The internal state is stored in the given configuration object. The stored information must be sufficient to restore the local state.

      The internal state shall not be changed by the implementations.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null reference to a configration object, which state is modified accordingly to the local internal state.

    • getActiveStrategyProperties

      public StrategyProperties getActiveStrategyProperties()
      returns a shallow copy of the strategy properties

    • setActiveStrategyProperties

      public void setActiveStrategyProperties(StrategyProperties p)
      sets the strategy properties if different from current ones

    • getTimeout

      public long getTimeout()
      retrieves the time in ms after which automatic rule application shall be aborted (-1 disables timeout)
      Returns:
      time in ms after which automatic rule application shall be aborted

    • setTimeout

      public void setTimeout(long timeout)
      sets the time after which automatic rule application shall be aborted (-1 disables timeout)
      Parameters:
      timeout - a long specifying the timeout in ms

    • getApplyStrategyStopCondition

      public StopCondition<Goal> getApplyStrategyStopCondition()

      Returns the StopCondition to use in an ApplyStrategy instance to determine after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or not.

      By default is an AppliedRuleStopCondition used which stops the auto mode if the given maximal number of rule applications or a defined timeout is reached. If a customized implementation is defined for the current proof via setCustomApplyStrategyStopCondition(StopCondition) this instance is returned instead.

      Returns:
      The StopCondition to use in an ApplyStrategy instance.

    • getCustomApplyStrategyStopCondition

      public StopCondition<Goal> getCustomApplyStrategyStopCondition()
      Returns a customized StopCondition which is used in an ApplyStrategy to determine after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or not.
      Returns:
      The customized StopCondition or null if the default one should be used.

    • setCustomApplyStrategyStopCondition

      public void setCustomApplyStrategyStopCondition(StopCondition<Goal> customApplyStrategyStopCondition)
      Defines the StopCondition which is used in an ApplyStrategy to determine after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or not.
      Parameters:
      customApplyStrategyStopCondition - The customized StopCondition to use or null to use the default one.

    • getCustomApplyStrategyGoalChooser

      public GoalChooser<Proof,Goal> getCustomApplyStrategyGoalChooser()
      Returns the customized GoalChooser which is used in an ApplyStrategy instance to select the next Goal to apply a rule on. If no one is defined the default one of the ApplyStrategy, which is defined by the user interface, is used.
      Returns:
      The customized GoalChooser to use or null to use the default one of the ApplyStrategy.

    • setCustomApplyStrategyGoalChooser

      public void setCustomApplyStrategyGoalChooser(GoalChooser<Proof,Goal> customGoalChooser)
      Sets the customized GoalChooser which is used in an ApplyStrategy instance to select the next Goal to apply a rule on. If no one is defined the default one of the ApplyStrategy, which is defined by the user interface, is used.
      Parameters:
      customGoalChooser - The customized GoalChooser to use or null to use the default one of the ApplyStrategy.
